title: Unify active transaction selection around exact active_tx identity
goal: Ensure the runtime always selects the active transaction by exact canonical active_tx identity from materialized state, and never by heuristic reconstruction, timestamp-first choice, sentinel interpretation, or session-led selection.
why_this_ticket: The 0.6.0 redesign depends on deterministic resume of the exact active transaction. This ticket converts active transaction selection from potentially ambiguous or artifact-driven behavior into exact canonical selection rooted in the materialized continuation contract.
scope: Review every runtime code path that selects, infers, or reconstructs the active transaction.
Identify any places where active transaction selection depends on string-shape checks, reverse mapping, timestamps, session context, sentinel IDs, or helper-style labels.
Update runtime selection logic so active transaction choice is based on exact active_tx identity from canonical materialized state.
Ensure no-active state is handled structurally through active_tx: null rather than fake identifiers or heuristic fallback.
Ensure rebuild paths preserve exact active transaction identity when materialized state is missing, incomplete, or inconsistent.
Document any bounded legacy compatibility behavior retained for historical data without weakening the new exact-selection rule.
out_of_scope: Redesigning the event log format beyond what is needed to preserve exact active transaction recovery.
Broad test-suite expansion beyond the minimum needed to validate exact active selection behavior.
General refactoring unrelated to active transaction selection.
expected_outputs: A code-level map of all active transaction selection paths.
A list of heuristic or ambiguous selection mechanisms removed or replaced.
An implementation update that selects the active transaction only from exact canonical active_tx identity.
A short implementation note describing any bounded historical compatibility behavior used during rebuild or read paths.
rules: Active transaction selection must prefer healthy materialized state over heuristic reconstruction.
If active_tx is null, no active transaction exists.
If active_tx is non-null, selection must target that exact active transaction identity, including tx_id.
Selection must not depend on string-prefix or substring checks over transaction identifiers.
Selection must not depend on sentinel transaction identifiers.
Selection must not depend on timestamp-first ordering.
Selection must not depend on session-parent modeling or session-led identity recovery.
When rebuild is required, canonical event-log replay may recover the exact active transaction, but it must not mint a substitute transaction or choose among heuristic candidates.
Compatibility handling for historical data may normalize documented legacy formats, but it must not weaken exact active transaction selection for new runtime behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Runtime code selects the active transaction by exact canonical active_tx identity when materialized state is healthy.
No code path selects an active transaction by timestamp-first choice, string heuristics, sentinel interpretation, or helper-label reverse mapping.
No-active state is represented structurally and handled as absence of active_tx rather than as a fake transaction identifier.
Rebuild paths preserve exact active transaction identity instead of minting or inferring replacement identities.
Session context is not used as the primary basis for active transaction selection.
The resulting behavior matches the plan: resume always continues the same active transaction.
review_checkpoints: Confirm that every entrypoint deciding between begin and resume uses exact active_tx identity.
Confirm that any fallback or rebuild path preserves exact tx_id continuity.
Confirm that no helper-style label can redefine which transaction is active.
Confirm that historical compatibility logic remains read-path-bounded and does not alter the new canonical selection rule.
notes: This ticket is central to deterministic resume and should be treated as a correctness boundary, not a cosmetic cleanup.
When in doubt, remove ambiguous selection logic rather than adding more tie-breaking heuristics.
All implementation decisions in this ticket must remain consistent with the minimal canonical continuation contract defined in plan.md.
